Git Workflow Guide: Git is a distributed version control system that tracks changes to source code over time and enables collaboration across distributed teams. This guide covers the feature-branch workflow: creating branches for each task, committing with descriptive messages, opening pull requests for code review, and merging via squash or rebase. We explain the difference between merge, rebase, and cherry-pick, and when each is appropriate. Best practices for commit message formatting following the Conventional Commits specification are also covered.
